summary:
Technology alone is insufficient to improve glycaemic control in type 1 diabetes; effective outcomes depend on established medical treatments such as precise insulin dosing, rigorous glucose targeting, patient education, and comprehensive healthcare coordination beyond mere use of digital tools. The core drivers of improved metabolic health remain clinically grounded practices rather than technology itself.  

(Word count: 86)

The summary provided by Agent2 correctly identifies that while telemedicine enables remote access, it lacks standalone efficacy in improving HbA1c or blood sugar levels without concurrent improvements in treatment protocols—such as consistent insulin administration—and holistic patient-centered strategies including behavioral change and professional oversight. Thus, technology serves a supportive role within broader therapeutic frameworks where traditional medicine remains central.
